name: wifi-jammer
summary: Repository indexing entry for the ``wifi-jammer`` project. Tool in the Wireless category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Detected in source; description must come from project docs.

arguments.name: --interactive
arguments.description: Detected in source; description must come from project docs.

arguments.name: --non-interactive
arguments.description: Detected in source; description must come from project docs.

arguments.name: --world
arguments.description: Detected in source; description must come from project docs.

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --port
arguments.description: Target port (default: service default).

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --threads
arguments.description: Number of concurrent workers.

arguments.name: --timeout
arguments.description: Per-operation timeout in seconds.

arguments.name: --rate
arguments.description: Packet / request rate limit (packets/sec).

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

arguments.name: --user-agent
arguments.description: HTTP User-Agent header value.

arguments.name: --cookie
arguments.description: HTTP Cookie header value.

arguments.name: --header
arguments.description: Extra HTTP header in key:value form (repeatable).

arguments.name: --insecure
arguments.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (lab use only).

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Emit JSON output instead of human-readable text.

arguments.name: --no-color
arguments.description: Disable ANSI color in output.

arguments.name: --quiet
arguments.description: Suppress non-error output.

arguments.name: --yes
arguments.description: Skip confirmation prompts (use with care).

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

function_signatures.name: APs_add
function_signatures.signature: def APs_add(clients_APs, APs, pkt, chan_arg, world_arg)

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with worknshubh/wifi-sukuna (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with tdiprima/wifi-is-radio (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with t6x/reaver-wps-fork-t6x (score=10); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['wifi_2_4_ghz', 'wifi_5_ghz', 'shell_linux'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 4: same attack_surface=['wifi_2_4_ghz', 'wifi_5_ghz', 'shell_linux'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 5: same attack_surface=['wifi_2_4_ghz', 'wifi_5_ghz', 'shell_linux'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use this tool when the engagement is in the 'any' phase against a target with '['wifi_2_4_ghz', 'wifi_5_ghz', 'shell_linux']' as the attack surface (wireless, toolbox, github).
documentation.why_to_use: This tool is preferred because: Index entry in the Wireless category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner after a NVD/lookup pass on the target vendor.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: Index entry in the Wireless category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner after a NVD/lookup pass on the target vendor.
Pairs with the Wireless system-prompt stanza in core.ai_backend.chain to teach the LLM the standard invocation pattern.
Operator-initiated: the chain step is gated by the per-step ACCEPT/CANCEL gate; the tool does not auto-execute.
Use as a reference implementation when designing a new v3 method for this category.
Use as a teaching example in the prompt stanza so the LLM can reason about the right run order.
